user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator's control intent becomes one durable, idempotent operation whose lifecycle is visible through an append-only public-safe event stream.
rationale: MCP, TUI, and loop code currently perform independent mutations or emit best-effort audit rows, so retry, recovery, monitoring, and later web controls cannot agree on one operation. This slice owns control-command-v1 and operation-event-v1 from #109. It creates the transport-neutral service that resolves #68's dark pause engine only after adapters in S06 make the command reachable.
in_scope: Define and validate control-command-v1 with operation ID, idempotency key, actor, target, kind, expected status revision, request time, and authorization context.
Define operation-event-v1 with repository-local sequence, stable event/operation IDs, actor, target, kind, safe detail, and source status revision.
Migrate the repository-local SQLite store with commands, operations, and versioned operation_events tables while retaining existing supervisor data.
Implement atomic Submit, Claim, Complete, Reject, and List/Get operations with typed invalid, conflict, busy, and storage errors.
Support pause, resume, retry, defer, acknowledge, and merge-approval intent as validated command kinds without granting automatic production merge authority.
Make loop/scheduler command polling claim each accepted operation once and append explicit accepted, started, succeeded, rejected, or failed events.
Redact or reject credentials, model payloads, request bodies, response bodies, and local worktree paths from public-safe event detail.
out_of_scope: Writing status through compare-and-write is S05-revisioned-state-ownership.
CLI, MCP, and TUI transport adapters are S06-control-adapter-parity.
HTTP/SSE and web authentication are S09-operations-read-api and S11-authenticated-remote-controls.
Automatic integration merge remains prohibited until S12's merge-delegation-v1 contract is present.
The twelve planned 2026-07-11-contract-edge-gates slices remain excluded.

effort_complexity.rationale: This introduces a durable application-service boundary, a versioned schema migration, concurrent command claiming, event safety, and loop integration.

risks.description: Command submission and its accepted event diverge during a partial database failure.
risks.mitigation: Write command, operation, and accepted event in one SQLite transaction and inject failures before commit.

risks.description: Concurrent pollers execute one command more than once.
risks.mitigation: Claim with a conditional state transition in one transaction and test two independent database connections racing the same operation.

risks.description: Free-form event detail leaks a secret or machine-local path to the later web/notification plane.
risks.mitigation: Prefer typed detail fields, validate safe projections at append time, and include sentinel secret/path rejection tests.

risks.description: A schema migration makes existing supervisor databases unreadable.
risks.mitigation: Upgrade a version-1 fixture in place and prove track, decision, event, and circuit data remain queryable.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN a valid control-command-v1 is submitted THE SYSTEM SHALL atomically persist one pending operation and one accepted operation-event-v1 (C-02, C-03, N-02).
WHEN the same idempotency key is submitted again for the same actor and target THE SYSTEM SHALL return the original operation without appending another accepted event (C-02, C-03, N-02).
IF a command's expected status revision is stale THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject it with a typed conflict before mutation (C-02, N-02).
WHEN two command processors race to claim one pending operation THE SYSTEM SHALL grant exactly one claim (C-02, C-03, N-02).
WHEN operation events are appended concurrently THE SYSTEM SHALL expose a unique monotonically ordered repository-local sequence (C-03, N-02).
IF event detail contains a credential sentinel, model payload, request body, response body, or local worktree path THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL reject the event before storage (C-03, N-02).
WHEN an existing version-1 supervisor database is opened THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve its rows while adding the command and operation-event schema (C-02, C-03, N-02).
